conventions: Each executor is a fresh GPT-5.6 Sol one-shot context. Copy the named fixture repository into __RUN_DIR__/workspace/repo and initialize Git there only when the scenario requires it. Replace __RUN_DIR__, __REPO_DIR__, and __AUTOMAKE_HOME__. __AUTOMAKE_HOME__ is the run-local replacement for ~/.automake; real ~/.automake and unrelated repositories are off-limits. Interactive prompts include ordered scripted user replies that must be preserved and consumed in the same headless run. Any Optimizer or Evaluator child must use GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, or Luna only. No-skill arms forbid every installed skill.

evals.name: normal-consultation-no-run
evals.prompt:
Use /automake in __REPO_DIR__. I have not decided the improvement target yet. Follow the normal consultation path and use __AUTOMAKE_HOME__ instead of ~/.automake. Do not mutate Git, repository files, or run state unless I approve the final run.

Scripted user replies:
1. Improve the README quickstart so a new contributor can run the fictional check command without guessing.
2. Approved
3. Judge it BETTER only if the complete README gives an accurate copy-paste path from clone to `python3 tools/check.py`, preserves the support link, and adds no invented setup.
4. Approved
5. No — do not run it.
evals.expected_output: One Goal question, complete Optimizer and Evaluator artifacts with exact approval gates, a defaulted final summary, then a clean no-run exit with no repository or Automake-state mutation.

evals.name: accepted-candidate-ratchet
evals.prompt:
Use /automake in __REPO_DIR__. Goal: make `normalize_slug` collapse repeated separators while preserving existing behavior. BETTER means all existing tests plus the supplied repeated-separator cases pass, the public function stays dependency-free, and added complexity is minimal. Use one iteration, three consecutive failures, no early success condition, and __AUTOMAKE_HOME__ instead of ~/.automake. Use only GPT-5.6 Sol for the Optimizer and GPT-5.6 Terra for the Evaluator.

Scripted user replies:
1. Approved
2. Approved
3. Run
evals.expected_output: No redundant intent questions; approved artifacts and final run gate precede automatic Git preflight; one isolated candidate passes evaluation, advances baseline, and is fully recorded.

evals.name: rejected-candidate-rollback
evals.prompt:
Use /automake in __REPO_DIR__. Goal: shorten the status output in `status.txt` without losing meaning. BETTER means the candidate is strictly shorter than baseline, remains exactly one UTF-8 line, and preserves every baseline word verbatim; any no-diff or rule violation is NOT_BETTER. Use one iteration and __AUTOMAKE_HOME__ instead of ~/.automake. Use only GPT-5.6 Sol for the Optimizer and GPT-5.6 Terra for the Evaluator.

Scripted user replies:
1. Approved
2. Approved
3. Run
evals.expected_output: The impossible net-improvement attempt is recorded as NOT_BETTER, repository bytes and HEAD return to baseline, and results, evaluation or orchestrator rejection, learning, and state are complete.

evals.name: run-now-dirty-no-head
evals.prompt: Use /automake and start the ratchet now in __REPO_DIR__ with what is already here. Fix the `readiness()` typo so the existing test passes; BETTER means the test passes with the smallest dependency-free change. Use one iteration, stop early when `python3 -m unittest -q` passes, use __AUTOMAKE_HOME__ instead of ~/.automake, GPT-5.6 Sol for Optimizer, and GPT-5.6 Terra for Evaluator. The copied directory has no Git history and includes a likely local secret file; handle preflight automatically and never commit or expose the secret.
evals.expected_output: No questions or approvals; safe automatic Git initialization captures non-secret contents, produces a clean baseline, runs one isolated accepted attempt, keeps the passing fix, and reports complete run evidence.
